message:
fix(python): GED edge subst cost ≤ delete+insert

In _graph_edit_exact_paths_python, the per-edge substitution-cost
branch unconditionally applied edge_subst_cost when both endpoints
existed in G2. With non-default cost callbacks the substitution
can be more expensive than delete+insert, which inflates total
cost above the true optimum.

Take min(subst_cost, delete_cost + insert_cost) so the local solver
remains a lower bound when callable costs are supplied.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -29430,14 +29430,20 @@ def evaluate(mapping):
             right_v = mapping.get(left_v)
             if right_u is not None and right_v is not None and G2.has_edge(right_u, right_v):
                 right_edge = (right_u, right_v)
-                cost += _graph_edit_edge_subst_cost(
+                subst_cost = _graph_edit_edge_subst_cost(
                     G1,
                     G2,
                     left_edge,
                     right_edge,
                     edge_match,
                     edge_subst_cost,
                 )
+                edit_as_delete_insert = _graph_edit_edge_del_cost(
+                    G1,
+                    left_edge,
+                    edge_del_cost,
+                ) + _graph_edit_edge_ins_cost(G2, right_edge, edge_ins_cost)
+                cost += min(subst_cost, edit_as_delete_insert)
                 matched_right_edges.add(_graph_edit_distance_edge_key(G2, right_edge))
             else:
                 cost += _graph_edit_edge_del_cost(G1, left_edge, edge_del_cost)
